text.title: Stripe Webhook Handler Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Stripe Webhook Handler Pattern methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Handler MUST call the Stripe SDK signature verification helper BEFORE any business logic; verification failure MUST return 400 without leaking error detail.
rule.rationale: Without verification the endpoint is a public DoS / injection surface; verification is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Handler MUST deduplicate by Stripe `event.id` against a persistent store (DB unique index or Redis SETNX); duplicates MUST be skipped, not re-processed.
rule.rationale: Stripe retries on 5xx and on slow 2xx; without idempotency duplicates re-process payments and double-fulfill orders.

rule.statement: Handler MUST return 2xx within 5 seconds; heavy work MUST go to a queue (SQS / Celery / pg-boss) and return immediately.
rule.rationale: Stripe times out at 30s and retries; slow handlers create retry storms and duplicate processing.

rule.statement: Failed processing MUST be written to a dead-letter queue or table with the full event payload; bare 5xx that relies on Stripe retries is rejected because Stripe gives up after 3 days.
rule.rationale: Stripe retries 3 days then gives up; without DLQ failed events are silently lost.

rule.statement: Handler MUST be replay-safe: re-processing the same event id (after a manual replay) MUST converge to the same end state with no extra side effect.
rule.rationale: Ops occasionally replay; non-replay-safe handlers diverge state and require hand reconciliation.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if every entry in `## Applies If` holds and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: Misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch.
